items.q: How do you say 'to Spain' (a feminine country)?
items.why: Students use 'à' or 'au'; feminine countries take 'en', e.g. en Espagne, en France.
Students mistranslate it; 'à l'étranger' means abroad, central to the international-world theme.
Students don't recognise francophone countries; French is widely spoken in parts of Africa such as Senegal.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going on holiday to Spain'?
items.correct: Je vais en vacances en Espagne.
items.why: It's Je vais en vacances en Espagne.. “Je suis vacances Espagne.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Global warming. “A summer holiday” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: à l'étranger. “à la maison” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
